protocol.name: kde_screencast_unstable_v2
protocol.copyright:
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2020-2021 Aleix Pol Gonzalez <aleixpol@kde.org> SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2026 David Redondo <kde@david-redondo.de>
SPDX-License-Identifier: LGPL-2.1-or-later

interface.name: kde_screencast_manager_v2

description.summary: protocol for managing PipeWire feeds of the different displays and windows

interface.description: Warning! The protocol described in this file is a desktop environment implementation detail. Regular clients must not use this protocol. Backward incompatible changes may be added without bumping the major version of the extension.

entry.name: embedded
entry.summary: Render the cursor on the stream

entry.name: metadata
entry.summary: Send metadata about where the cursor is through PipeWire

arg.name: output
arg.summary: the name of the output to stream

description.summary: stream a single window including its popups
stream a region of the entire workspace

arg.name: x
arg.summary: X coordinate of the top left corner in logical coordinates

arg.name: y
arg.summary: Y coordinate of the top left corner in logical coordinates

description.summary: Create and stream a new virtual output
set the pointer mode to use for the stream
set the pointer mode to use for the stream
set the pointer mode to use for the stream

request.name: set_scale

description.summary: set the scale to use for this stream

request.description: If not provided or set to 0, the compositor will choose a suitable scale factor.

description.summary: set the pointer mode to use for the stream
set the name of the created output
set the user visible description of the created output
Notifies that the server has stopped the stream. Clients should now delete the stream.
Notifies about a pipewire feed being created

arg.name: object_serial_hi
arg.summary: high bits of the pipewire object serial

arg.name: object_serial_low
arg.summary: low bits of the pipewire object serial

description.summary: Creation of the stream failed. Clients should noew delete the stream.

arg.name: error
arg.summary: A human readable translated error message.
